$comment: Ch 2 — THE SPIN, scenario 2 (heavy backspin / the shooter's roll). Authored from SCENARIO_SPECS_v1.md + SCENARIO_VOICE_v1.md (locked 2026-05-21). Schema mirrors bb-1-baseline.json. Spin simulated via lesson copy + visual treatment (the answer is intentionally a higher-arc shot, which in real basketball is the type of shot backspin would tend to save).

meta.title: THE SHOOTER'S ROLL.
meta.subtitle: CH 2 — THE SPIN, HEAVY BACKSPIN
meta.authorIntent: A midrange jumper with the shooter's-roll framing. d=5.0, h_r=2.2, h_h=3.05. Textbook answer θ=58°, v=7.81 m/s. Verification: t = 5.0 / (7.81·cos 58°) = 1.208 s; y(1.208) = 2.2 + 7.81·sin(58°)·1.208 − ½·9.8·1.208² = 3.050 m — matches h_h (err 0.000 m). Higher-arc answer (58° vs 52° baseline) — geometrically the 'softer' shot, the one a backspin-rich release would land more reliably. Lesson copy ties the higher arc to 'soft hands give the ball lift' — the user learns that the shooter's roll shows up in our model as: pick the higher-arc solution and you have more margin.

situation.questionRevealed: Find an angle θ and velocity v that lands the ball through the hoop.

subheadVariants.1: Came up short. Rotation needs a release that reaches.
subheadVariants.2: Still short. The arc needs more height.

subheadVariants.1: Front iron. Spin couldn't save a flat arc.
subheadVariants.2: Still front iron. Lift the arc.
subheadVariants.3+: Front iron again. Push the angle higher.

subheadVariants.1: Back iron. Too much heat for the rim to hold.
subheadVariants.2: Still back iron. Cut the velocity.

subheadVariants.1: Long. Spin doesn't undo extra velocity.
subheadVariants.3+: Far overshoot. Power well above threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Air ball. Rotation, but never on line.
subheadVariants.2: Air ball again. Aim for the rim.
subheadVariants.3+: Still air ball. Check angle and velocity.

ghostArc.description: Textbook answer θ=58°, v=7.81 m/s. The arc visibly crests higher than the baseline — this is the geometric expression of 'soft hands' that the chapter is teaching. Reveal copy ties the higher-arc solution to backspin's forgiveness in the real game.

hints.body: Backspin lives outside the equations. Solve the projectile. Higher arc = more forgiveness.

Two equations, two unknowns:
  x(t) = v·cos(θ)·t
  y(t) = h_r + v·sin(θ)·t − ½·g·t²
Aim for a higher arc this time.
Substitute t = d/(v·cos θ) into y. Try θ near 58° — higher than baseline — and solve for v.

workedSteps: Solve y(t) = h_h for v → v ≈ 7.81 m/s.

intro.headline: THE SHOOTER'S ROLL.
intro.subhead: Heavy backspin. The iron has options.

subheadByFlavor.SWISH: Spin did its job. Net never moved twice.
subheadByFlavor.RIM_DROP: Back iron, dies, falls. That's the roll.

miss.headline: ROLLED OFF

diagnosticByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Front iron. Spin couldn't save a flat arc.
diagnosticByCategory.BACK_RIM: Back iron. Too much heat for the rim to hold.
diagnosticByCategory.SHORT: Came up short. Rotation needs a release that reaches.
diagnosticByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Long. Spin doesn't undo extra velocity.
diagnosticByCategory.AIRBALL: Air ball. Rotation, but never on line.

bracketHintByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Geometry: θ near 58°, push v near 7.8.
bracketHintByCategory.BACK_RIM: Geometry: θ near 58°, cut v by ~0.4.
bracketHintByCategory.SHORT: Geometry: θ near 58°, push v near 7.8.
bracketHintByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Geometry: θ near 58°, cut v by ~0.8.

miss.afterAllHintsCopy: All hints revealed. The math is on the table.
